title: A Quiet Place: Day One Opening Day Box Office Performance
text: A Quiet Place: Day One achieved a record-breaking opening day for the franchise. The Paramount horror sequel earned $22.5 million from 3,708 theaters across Friday and preview screenings, surpassing the 2021 sequel's single-day haul of $19.3 million. The film's strong performance positions it well for its opening weekend, with projections reaching approximately $53 million potentially exceeding the original film's franchise record of $50.2 million. The prequel, directed by Michael Sarnoski and starring Lupita Nyong'o and Joseph Quinn, received positive early audience reception with a B+ Cinema Score rating. The production carried a $67 million budget and benefited from premium format screenings across IMAX and large-format theaters. Critical reception was solid, contributing to the robust ticket sales during its debut.
